page: Microsoft Azure FX4mds_v2 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the FXmdsv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs derived from 2 physical Intel Xeon 8573C cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor operates at a base speed of 4.2 GHz and includes a 260 MB L3 cache. Memory capacity is 84.0 GB, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 21.0 GB per core. The server includes 236 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, though it does not feature any GPUs.

Benchmark results show strong single-core performance, with Geekbench single-core and text processing scores reaching top-tier levels. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also register strong performance. However, multi-core scaling is limited by the physical core count, resulting in weak multi-core CPU performance in stress-ng and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Memory bandwidth is average for cached small blocks but drops to weak for uncached large blocks. Database operations and Redis SET operations perform in the average tier, while LLM inference speeds range from average for small models to poor for larger models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the FX4mds_v2 offers high memory density per vCPU, making it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require massive parallel processing. The presence of dedicated high-frequency Intel Xeon cores with advanced vector and matrix extensions supports specialized mathematical and cryptographic operations. This server is best suited for memory-intensive applications, relational databases, caching layers, and single-threaded data processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure FX4mds_v2 is a memory-intensive x86_64 server powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical cores, 84.0 GB of RAM, and 236 GB of local storage. The server delivers top-tier single-core CPU and text processing performance, alongside strong single-threaded compression speeds. However, multi-core performance and uncached memory bandwidth are relatively weak due to the 2-core physical limit. With a high memory-to-core ratio of 21.0 GB per core and advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX, this server is designed for memory-heavy workloads, database operations, and single-threaded processing tasks where high memory density is required without massive parallel compute overhead.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX4mds_v2 is a memory-intensive server featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 84.0 GB of RAM, and 236 GB of local storage, optimized for single-core efficiency and database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure FX4mds_v2 is a memory-optimized server with 4 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 84.0 GB RAM, and 236 GB local storage for database workloads.
tagline: A memory-dense virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processing and local storage for memory-intensive and single-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs operating at 4.2 GHz with 260 MB L3 cache
84.0 GB of RAM providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 21.0 GB per core
236 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Support for advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and AMX
Best suited for memory-intensive databases, caching, and text processing
